popularStory.headline: Thomas Brooks
popularStory.description: Thomas Brooks was the first of the name in New England. he was born in 1610 and is said to have come from Suffolk, England and sailed in the "Susan and Ellen" to Watertown, Mass. here he was assigned …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Brooks於1595年3月2日在英國藍卡爾曼徹斯特受洗時，他的父親Thomas Brooke27歲，母親Katheryn27歲。他於1618年2月2日與Grace Cunliffe在英國藍卡爾曼徹斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1667年5月21日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可康可德享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Brooks于1595年3月2日在英国藍卡爾曼徹斯特受洗时，他的父亲Thomas Brooke27岁，母亲Katheryn27岁。他于1618年2月2日与Grace Cunliffe在英国藍卡爾曼徹斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1667年5月21日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可康可德享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Brooks
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually a variant of Brook , with excrescent -s. The optional addition of -s, with no grammatical function, is usually post-medieval, but some examples of the same person's name occurring with and without -(e)s have been noted as early as the 14th century in South Lancashire. The -es in such cases probably has neither a plural nor a genitival function, and the name means ‘dweller at the brook’, not ‘dweller at the brooks’. A plural sense cannot be ruled out elsewhere, but a non-grammatical -(e)s must also be considered a strong possibility.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Brucks
meanings: probably a habitational name from Brucks (also Brux) which derives from Middle Low German brōk ‘low-lying marsh’.
reportedly also an altered form of Flemish (Dutch) Broex, an old variant of Broeks (see Brooks ).

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Brooks was christened on 2 March 1595, in Manchester, Lancashire, England, his father, Thomas Brooke, was 27 and his mother, Katheryn, was 27. He married Grace Cunliffe on 2 February 1618, in Manchester, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 21 May 1667, in Concord, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 72.

ko.Concord, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 콘코르드
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Brooks(이)가 1595년 3월 2일 영국 랭카실르 먼체스터에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Brooke(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Katheryn(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1618년 2월 2일 영국 랭카실르 먼체스터에서 Grace Cunliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1667년 5월 21일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 콘코르드에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Brooks foi batizado em 2 de março de 1595, em Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Brooke, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Katheryn, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Grace Cunliffe em 2 de fevereiro de 1618, em Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 21 de maio de 1667, em Concord, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Brooks был крещен 2 марта 1595 г. в местности манчестир, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Brooke было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Katheryn, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace Cunliffe 2 февраля 1618 г. в местности манчестир, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 21 мая 1667 г. в местности конкорд, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 72 лет.
